No Poverty.reason: The course on international investment and global monetary economics can indirectly contribute to poverty reduction by improving access to financial services (Target 1.4) and fostering pro-poor investment policies (Target 1.b).

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies relates to food production, access, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies relates to health, disease prevention, or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As an undergraduate university course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops professional knowledge and skills for employment (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: The syllabus mentions 'socially responsible issues' but provides no specific evidence or focus on gender equality or related targets.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies relates to water resources, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly addresses 'International Investment' and 'global monetary economics,' which are central to sustaining economic growth (Target 8.1) and strengthening financial institutions (Target 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: International investment is a key driver for developing quality infrastructure (Target 9.1, 9.a) and providing financial access for industrial enterprises (Target 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course on global monetary economics and financial markets directly relates to regulating global financial markets (Target 10.5) and encouraging financial flows to developing countries (Target 10.b) to reduce inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies relates to urban planning, housing, or sustainable communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course's core competence to 'understand and identify ethical, socially responsible, and sustainability issues' directly supports encouraging sustainable practices in companies through investment (Target 12.6).
evidence: To understand and identify ethical, socially responsible, and sustainability issues

Climate Action.reason: The course's focus on 'International Investment' and 'sustainability issues' provides an inferred link to mobilizing finance for climate action (Target 13.a).

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies relates to marine life, ocean conservation, or water ecosystems.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's focus on global monetary economics and financial markets provides an inferred link to developing transparent institutions (Target 16.6) and strengthening global economic governance (Target 16.8).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course on international investment and global monetary economics is central to global financial partnerships, mobilizing resources (Target 17.3), promoting investment (Target 17.5), and enhancing macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13).
